schema.levels: object with keys 1, 2, 3; each level contains exactly three required main quests

intro.title: Meet Chrome DevTools
intro.description: Study an introductory reference for Chrome DevTools, then summarize what it is, when it is used, and one small practical action a beginner can try.

1.title: Identify Chrome DevTools Basics
1.description: Read the beginner material for Chrome DevTools, then explain the core terms a new teammate must understand before using it.

1.title: Map a Simple Chrome DevTools Use Case
1.description: Describe a small real-world situation where Chrome DevTools helps, including the input, action, and expected result.

1.title: Avoid a Common Chrome DevTools Mistake
1.description: Find one beginner mistake related to Chrome DevTools, then summarize how to notice it and how to avoid it.

2.title: Apply Chrome DevTools in Context
2.description: Use the reference to design a practical workflow for Chrome DevTools, then explain why each step is needed.

2.title: Compare Chrome DevTools Tradeoffs
2.description: Write a comparison between a simple approach and a more structured approach for Chrome DevTools, including when each is appropriate.

2.title: Troubleshoot Chrome DevTools
2.description: Create a short troubleshooting checklist for Chrome DevTools, focusing on symptoms, likely causes, and safe next steps.

3.title: Design a Reusable Chrome DevTools Practice
3.description: Turn Chrome DevTools into a repeatable practice that another teammate could follow, including review points and quality checks.

3.title: Review Chrome DevTools Like a Senior
3.description: Audit a Chrome DevTools decision for correctness, maintainability, and risk, then summarize what you would approve or change.

3.title: Prove Chrome DevTools Mastery
3.description: Create a small proof-of-understanding artifact for Chrome DevTools, such as notes, a checklist, a mini demo, or a before-and-after explanation. Show how browser inspection reveals HTML, CSS, JavaScript, network, and performance behavior.

intro.title: Open the Editor Workspace
intro.description: Learn the VS Code workspace basics, then summarize what panels, files, and commands a beginner should recognize first.

1.title: Navigate and Edit Files
1.description: Study basic editing and navigation, then describe how VS Code helps someone move through project files without losing context.

1.title: Use Built-in Source Control
1.description: Learn the Source Control view, then summarize how VS Code exposes changed files, staged files, and commit messages.

1.title: Adjust Basic Settings
1.description: Review settings, then write which editor preferences are safe for personal workflow and which should be shared carefully with a team.

2.title: Debug a Program Flow
2.description: Study debugging concepts, then summarize how breakpoints, variables, and call stacks help find a bug faster than reading code blindly.

2.title: Run a Repeatable Task
2.description: Learn VS Code tasks, then describe a task that would save repeated terminal work in a real project.

2.title: Choose Extensions Deliberately
2.description: Review the extension marketplace, then write a short policy for choosing useful extensions without overloading the editor.

3.title: Organize a Multi-folder Workspace
3.description: Study workspaces, then explain how a multi-folder workspace can support a project with frontend, backend, and documentation areas.

3.title: Create a Profile for a Role
3.description: Learn profiles, then summarize how separate profiles can keep different job contexts or project types from polluting each other.

3.title: Automate Editor Launches
3.description: Review command-line usage, then describe how opening files, folders, or commands from the terminal can speed up troubleshooting.
